cvrp_construct.algorithm: At each step, choose the nearest feasible unvisited customer whose demand does not exceed remaining capacity; if no such customer exists or returning to the depot now yields a better savings than continuing, return to the depot early.

cvrp_construct.algorithm: The algorithm is a nearest-neighbor greedy heuristic that at each step selects the closest feasible unvisited customer; if no such customer exists, it returns to the depot.

```python
        return depot
    distances = distance_matrix[current_node][unvisited_nodes]
    nearest_idx = np.argmin(distances)
    nearest_node = unvisited_nodes[nearest_idx]
```
cvrp_construct.algorithm: The algorithm is a nearest-neighbor greedy heuristic that at each step selects the closest feasible unvisited customer while respecting vehicle capacity, returning to the depot when no more customers can be served.

cvrp_construct.algorithm: The heuristic starts each route by selecting the farthest feasible customer from the depot to encourage clustering towards distant areas, then switches to choosing the nearest feasible customer from the current position until the route is filled, finally returning to the depot when no more feasible customers remain.

cvrp_construct.algorithm: A hybrid two-phase greedy heuristic that seeds routes by selecting the farthest feasible customer from the depot when at or near the depot, then switches to selecting the nearest feasible customer from the current position while capacity allows, and returns to depot only when no feasible customers remain.

cvrp_construct.algorithm: Hybrid CVRP heuristic that uses far‑first clustering at the start of a route and regret‑based insertion while en route, always respecting capacity, to minimize total travel distance.

cvrp_construct.algorithm: A hybrid farthest-first with regret-based tie-breaking: start a route by targeting the farthest feasible cluster from the depot, then while on-route use a combination of nearest neighbor and regret insertion to prioritize customers with high future insertion cost, ensuring capacity feasibility throughout.

tsp_construct.algorithm: Iteratively select the next node by minimizing a weighted score combining normalized proximity to the current node, normalized inverse proximity to the nearest unvisited neighbor, and penalty for deviating from the straight-line direction toward the eventual destination node, then break ties by preferring nodes closer to the centroid of all remaining unvisited nodes.

tsp_construct.algorithm: Balanced Greedy Insertion with Forward Look: Selects the next node by minimizing a weighted combination of the direct distance from the current node and the estimated cost to re‑insert into the remaining tour toward the destination, breaking ties by preferring nodes closer to the destination.
